source_intake_record.raw_question_text: <image>The diameters of $\odot A, \odot B,$ and $\odot C$ are $8$ inches, $18$ inches, and $11$ inches, respectively. Find $FB$.
extraction_notes: image content is represented as PIL object strings rather than concrete file paths or URLs

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the diagram is central
The text references objects/points (circle A, circle B, circle C, and F) without defining their spatial relations in words
Finding FB from only circle diameters typically depends on diagram-specific geometry such as tangency or placement
multi_step: The problem likely requires converting diameters to radii and then using diagram-based geometric relations to derive FB
This is not a direct lookup; it appears to combine numerical values with implicit geometric structure from the figure
question_text is short, so some reasoning burden is likely offloaded to the image rather than explicit multi-condition text
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('5')
A geometry solution can usually be checked against the diagram and computed lengths
asset_registry_record.answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support stable downstream checking
